fix: Port TDW dict-order bug fix to hookimpl architecture (#1815)

After PR #1809 restructured the TDW workflow from sdk/python into
.midtown/channels/tdw/scripts/hooks.py, the original dict-order bug
fix needed to be ported to the new architecture.

Changes to hooks.py:
- Add ACTIVE_STAGES constant to replace duplicated tuples
- Fix _find_active_task_id to require exactly one active task (was
  returning first match based on arbitrary dict iteration order)
- Add _resolve_target_task for explicit task_id targeting via ctx
- Add _warn_no_target for user-facing ambiguity/inactive warnings
- Fix on_channel_message to use _resolve_target_task with ctx.task_id
- Fix on_coworker_message to use nested dict access consistently
  (was using dot-path keys that don't work with nested state dicts)

Changes to test_hooks.py:
- Fix test infrastructure to use HookContext instead of (event, ctx)
  two-arg calls that didn't match the hookimpl function signatures
- Add _apply_actions helper to execute set_state actions on state dict
- Add tests for dict-order ambiguity, explicit task_id targeting,
  inactive task warnings, and _resolve_target_task/_warn_no_target
- 44 tests (up from 32), all passing

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: Add embedded thread reply instructions to lead prompts [Midtown !2096] (#1817)

* docs: Add embedded thread reply instructions to lead prompts

When the daemon nudges a lead about an @mention from a thread, the nudge
includes explicit thread reply instructions (--thread <parent-id>). The
lead and channel-lead prompts only documented the (message-id) format
from the sender line, causing leads to reply at the top level instead of
in the correct thread.

Add a paragraph to both agents/lead.md and agents/channel-lead.md
explaining that embedded thread instructions take precedence over the
message-id in the sender line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Scope embedded thread instructions to root/fallback path

Clarify that embedded thread reply instructions in nudges only apply
in the root session or fallback path. Auto-forked sessions already
post to the correct thread automatically, so the instructions can be
ignored there. This resolves the placement tension between "just
respond" (auto-fork) and "always use these instructions."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use realistic XBID continuous market prices instead of ISP1 balancing

XBID settlement was using ISP1 up/down regulation prices (mean spread
156 EUR/MWh) which are TSO-activated balancing prices, not continuous
market prices. This caused the agent to learn unrealistic XBID arbitrage
(191k EUR/episode) that won't transfer to production.

New XBID pricing: DAM-based mid + 10% ISP1 influence + OU deviation,
with tight bid-ask spread (mean 3 EUR, range 1-8 EUR). ISP1 up/down
remain as observation features (balancing market signal).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix slider visibility and performance

commit.message:
Fix stale GCE encoding cache causing missing video files (#499)

## Summary
- When admin deletes outputs and re-triggers the video worker, the GCE
encoding worker returns a stale cached result (409/complete) but the
actual files no longer exist in GCS
- This caused YouTube upload to silently skip and the job to complete in
a half-published state (Dropbox has stems but no video, no YouTube
upload)
- Detected on job `f4a552bf` (Queen Anne - Let's Dance)

## Changes
- **Stale cache detection**: `_download_gce_encoded_files` now raises
`RuntimeError` when zero files are downloaded from GCS
- **Auto-retry with new job ID**: `_run_encoding` catches the stale
cache error and re-submits encoding with a suffixed job ID
(`{job_id}_retry_{uuid}`) so the GCE worker treats it as a fresh job
- **Distribution warning**: `_upload_to_youtube` now adds to
`distribution_warnings` when no video file is available (previously
silently returned)
- 3 new tests covering stale cache detection, retry logic, and YouTube
missing-file warning

## Test plan
- [x] All 57 orchestrator tests pass (54 existing + 3 new)
- [ ] Deploy and re-trigger job `f4a552bf` to verify fix in production

@coderabbitai ignore

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Restructure MOM as multi-crate Rust workspace with SurrealDB

**Architecture:**
- Workspace: mom-core, mom-store-surrealdb, mom-service, mom-cli
- mom-core: Stable kernel API (MemoryItem, Query, MemoryStore trait)
- mom-store-surrealdb: SurrealDB persistence with schema validation
- mom-service: Axum HTTP API with comprehensive endpoints
- mom-ts: Optional TypeScript/Bun client (dependency-free)

**HTTP Endpoints:**
- POST   /v1/memory    - Write events/facts
- GET    /v1/memory    - List with filtering
- GET    /v1/memory/:id - Get specific item
- DELETE /v1/memory/:id - Delete memory
- POST   /v1/recall    - Hybrid recall with scoring

**SurrealDB Features:**
- Schema validation at database layer
- Scope-aware queries (tenant/workspace/project/agent/run)
- RBAC permissions (future)
- Indexed time-range queries
- Full-text content search (MVP)

**TypeScript Client:**
- Standard fetch API (works Bun + Node)
- No dependencies
- Type-safe memory operations
- Helper scopes (default, agent-based)

Ready for Phase 2:
- Vector embeddings integration
- Advanced text search (FTS)
- Consolidation jobs
- MCP server bridge

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci(pre-commit): add check-merge-conflict and check-pixi-lock hooks

Add two new pre-commit hooks to prevent the top two CI failure causes
(53% stale pixi.lock, 13% merge conflict markers):

- check-pixi-lock: runs pixi install --locked when pyproject.toml/pixi.toml/pixi.lock
  change, catching stale lock files before they hit CI
- check-merge-conflict: detects <<<<<<< HEAD markers left by failed rebases,
  providing a clear error instead of cryptic ruff/mypy syntax failures

Also update CLAUDE.md Pre-commit Hooks section with explicit instructions
for auto-impl agents to run pre-commit before pushing and to regenerate
pixi.lock after any pyproject.toml/pixi.toml changes.

commit.message:
Fix Donna Hay image URLs (Donna Hay HTML bug workaround)

Donna Hay's og:image meta tag has a bug where their own domain is
prepended before the CDN URL, producing invalid URLs like
"http://donnahay.com.auhttps://cdn.donnahaycdn.com.au/...".

- fixDonnaHayImages.js: one-time script to repair all 303 existing
  recipes in donna-hay-recipes.json (all 303 were affected)
- scrapeDonnaHay.js: strip the leading domain segment on future runs
- testDonnaHayImage.js: diagnostic script confirming the bug source

commit.message:
fix(socket): add 10s reconnection grace period for mobile apps

- Add RECONNECT_GRACE_PERIOD config (10 seconds)
- Delay marking user offline until grace period expires
- Cancel offline timer if user reconnects within grace period
- Prevents rapid offline/online status flicker on mobile

Mobile apps frequently disconnect briefly when:
- App transitions to/from background
- Network switches (WiFi <-> cellular)
- OS battery optimization kicks in

This prevents false "user went offline" notifications.

feat: Add Unix socket server to Python workflow daemon [Midtown !2083] (#1812)

* feat: Add Unix socket server to Python workflow daemon

Replace WorkflowDaemon.run() placeholder with asyncio.start_unix_server()
listener. Newline-delimited JSON protocol with {"ready":true} handshake.

- Receive event JSON from Rust, dispatch to pluggy hooks, serialize
  action results back
- Hot-reload plugins before each dispatch
- Clean up stale socket files on startup

  without crashing the server

- Add pytest-asyncio dev dependency for async test support
- 9 new tests covering socket lifecycle, round-trip dispatch, error
  isolation, hot-reload during operation, and stale socket cleanup

fix: Show progress bar during author-to-reviewer transition (#1814)

The progress bar render condition required `hasProgress` (author's live
coworker progress != null), but when the author finishes and goes idle,
their progress becomes null — hiding the bar until the reviewer reports
state. Fix by also rendering when a reviewer exists, since
lifecycleSegments() already handles that case correctly (hardcodes
author at 70%).

commit.message:
refactor: Restructure TDW plugin into AgentSkills format [Midtown !2080] (#1809)

* refactor: Restructure TDW plugin into AgentSkills format

Move TDW workflow from monolithic sdk/python/midtown/tdw_workflow.py
into the AgentSkills directory structure at .midtown/channels/tdw/:

- .midtown/AGENTS.md: Workflow-agnostic facilitation instructions (HOW)
- .midtown/channels/tdw/SKILL.md: TDW-specific knowledge with
  frontmatter metadata (WHAT)
- .midtown/channels/tdw/scripts/hooks.py: @hookimpl handlers using
  context.rpc for state and returning DaemonAction lists
- .midtown/channels/tdw/scripts/test_hooks.py: Adapted tests (32 pass)

Key changes from old to new:
- Single handle() function -> separate @hookimpl functions
- Direct state dict -> context.rpc.get_state()/set_state()
- rpc.post_to_channel() calls -> Actions.post_to_channel() returns
- rpc.spawn_coworker() calls -> Actions.spawn_coworker() returns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Match hook signatures to SDK hookspecs and add missing hookspecs

- Fix pluggy parameter names: (event, context) -> (ctx) to match
  TaskHooks hookspec signatures. Pluggy matches by name, so the old
  signatures would silently fail dispatch.
- Add on_coworker_message and on_channel_message hookspecs to
  TaskHooks in the SDK.
- Add Actions.set_state() for pure state mutations via DaemonAction
  (replaces direct context.rpc.set_state() calls).
- Remove unused _state_key helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

blocked. Update the channel-lead prompt to allow Edit for notes/workflow

historically more prone to ignoring prompt-based restrictions (PR #1667).
Re-add Edit to the hard-block list for forks via a new
`channel_lead_fork_disallowed_tools()` function, while keeping it
available for top-level channel lead sessions that need it for notes.

Addresses review feedback from york on PR #1810.

commit.message:
fix(manage_experiment): skip null result_dir entries in --max-subtests expansion check

Entries in batch_summary.json with result_dir=null cannot be verified
for subtest count. Previously the code only entered the expansion check
when result_dir was truthy, so null entries fell through and incorrectly
added test_ids to completed_ids — even if a prior valid entry had
flagged them for re-expansion.

Fix: continue (skip) when result_dir is None, so null entries never
count as completed during a --max-subtests expansion run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
